title: The Disaster Management (Amendment) Act, 2026

sections.title: This Act may be cited as the Disaster Management
sections.body:
Short title
(Amendment) Act, 2026, and shall be read as one with the Disaster Cap. 132
Management Act, in this Act referred to as the principal Act.

sections.title: Section 2 of the principal Act is amended by the— Amendment
sections.body:
of section 2
(a) deletion of the—
(i)definition of “Unit”; and
(ii) definitions of “Council”, “District Committee”,
“District Development Coordinating Committee”,
“Provincial Committee”, “Provincial Development
Coordinating Committee”, “Satellite Committee”
and “Technical Committee” and the substitution
therefor of the following in the appropriate places
in alphabetical order:
“Council” means the National Disaster Risk
Management Council established under
section 8;
“District Committee” means the District
Disaster Risk Management Committee
constituted under section 21;
Single copies of this Act may be obtained from the Government Printer,
P. O. Box 30136, 10101 Lusaka. Price K24.00 each
38 No. 2 of 2026] Disaster Management (Amendment)
“District Development Coordinating
Committee” means the District
Development Coordinating Committee
constituted under the National Planning
Act No. 1 of
2020 and Budgeting Act, 2020;
“Provincial Committee”means the Provincial
Disaster Risk Management Committee
constituted under section 16;
“Provincial Development Coordinating
Committee” means the Provincial
Development Coordinating Committee
constituted under the National Planning
Act No.1 of
2020 and Budgeting Act, 2020;
“Satellite Committee” means the Satellite
Disaster Risk Management Committee
constituted under section 26; and
“Technical Committee” means the National
Disaster Risk Management Technical
Committee constituted under section
10;; and
(b) insertion of the following new definitions in the appropriate
places in alphabetical order:
“adaptation” has the meaning assigned to the
word in the Green Economy and
Act No. 18 of
2024 Climate Change Act, 2024;
“Division” means the National Disaster Risk
Management Division established
under section 4;
“disaster risk management plan” means a
plan for disaster prevention, mitigation,
preparedness, response and recovery;
“National Disaster Risk Management
Structure” means a structure designed
to minimise the impact of a disaster by
inte

sections.title: The principal Act is amended in Part II by the repeal of the Repeal and
sections.body:
heading and the substitution therefor of the following: replacement
of heading to
PART II
Part II
THE NATIONAL DISASTER RISK MANAGEMENT DIVISION

sections.title: The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section 4 Repeal and
sections.body:
replacement
and the substitution therefor of the following:
of section 4

sections.title: The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section 5 and Repeal and
sections.body:
the substitution therefor of the following: replacement
of section 5
5. (1) The Division shall be responsible for Functions of
implementing disaster risk management programmes Division
and activities in the Republic.
(2) Despite the generality of subsection (1),
the functions of the Division are to—
(a) act as the central planning, coordinating
and monitoring institution, taking into
account potential disaster risks, for—
(i) the prevention and mitigation of a
disaster;
(ii) the preparedness and response to a
disaster; and
(iii) post-disaster recovery;
(b) advise the Technical Committee on
matters relating to disaster risk
management and management of
disaster relief operations;
(c) warn the public of an imminent disaster
and predict the effects of the disaster;
40 No. 2 of 2026] Disaster Management (Amendment)
(d) maintain a data collection and
dissemination system, national strategic
reserves of essential commodities and
equipment, for purposes of immediate
disaster relief;
(e) formulate disaster prevention,
mitigation, preparedness, response
and rehabilitation strategies and action
plans to meet foreseeablere quirements
in collaboration with Government
agencies, non-governmental organisations,
donor agencies and other cooperating
partners;
(f) prepare and update disaster risk
management plans and the supporting
disaster risk management manual;
(g) prepare an over-arching national disaster
contingency plan and coordinate cross
sectoral disaster contingency plans;
(h) establish an early warning system
covering all sectors and hazard sources
and maintain close links with different
institutions that provide early warning
services;
(i)develop and sustain viable and effective
structures and capacities at national,
provincial, district and satellite level;
(j)take necessary measures in order to
prevent, alleviate, contain and minimise
the effects of a disaster;
(k) conduct public and media briefings on
risks and disaster related programmes,
progress and constraints;
(l)

sections.title: Section 10 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion Amendment
sections.body:
of — of section 10
(a) the marginal note and the substitution therefor of the
following:
Constitution of National Disaster Risk Management
Technical Committee; and
(b) subsection (1) and the substitution therefor of the following:
(1) There is constituted the National
Disaster Risk Management Technical
Committee.

sections.title: The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section 11 Repeal and
sections.body:
and the substitution therefor of the following: replacement
of section 11

sections.title: The functions of the Technical Committee are Functions of
sections.body:
to— Technical
Committee
(a) recommend policy and programme
direction to the Council;
(b) co-ordinate the implementation of
the decisions of the Council, using
the sectoral skills and resources
of line ministries;
(c) supervise disaster risk management
activities in the Republic and
co-ordinate the disaster risk
management content of
development and reconstruction
programmes;
(d) supervise the work of the Division
in the monitoring and review of
disaster risk management plans;
(e) control disbursement of funds from
the Trust Fund;
44 No. 2 of 2026] Disaster Management (Amendment)
(f) formulate a unified national early
warning protocol to articulate
responsibilities of institutions
and agencies in charge of
meteorological, hydrological,
geological, biological and medical
services; and
(g) formulate a national early warning
disseminating strategy to co-
ordinate agencies that interact
with communities and economic
sectors.
Amendment 11. Section 13 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion
of section 13 of subsection (5) and the substitution therefor of the following:
(5) The Civil Service Commission shall
appoint, as public officers, other staff of the
Division necessary for the performance of the
functions of the Division.
Amendment 12. Section 14(1) of the principal Act is amended by the—
of section 14
(a) insertion of the following new paragraph immediately
after paragraph (i):
(j) proposed addresses, locations and coordinates
for resettlement schemes;; and
(b) renumbering of paragraphs (j) and (k) as paragraphs (k)
and (l), respectively.
Repeal and 13. The principal Act is amended in Part III by the repeal of

of heading
to Part III

PROVINCIAL DISASTER RISK MANAGEMENT COMMITTEES

sections.title: The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section Repeal and
sections.body:
19 and the substitution therefor of the following: replacement
of section
19
19. (1) The Civil Service Commission shall Provincial
appoint, as a public officer, a Provincial Disaster Risk Disaster Risk
Management Coordinator. Management
Coordinator
(2) The Provincial Disaster Risk Management
Coordinator shall provide technical advisory support
to a Provincial Committee, District Committee and
Satellite Committee.

sections.title: Section 21 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion Amendment
sections.body:
of — of section 21
(a) subsection (1) and the substitution therefor of the following:
(1) There is constituted a District Disaster
Risk Management Committee in each district which
shall be a sub-committee of the District Development
Coordinating Committee.;
(b) subsection (2) and the substitution therefor of the following:
(2) A District Committee consists of the
following part-time members appointed by the
Provincial Permanent Secretary:
(a) the District Commissioner, as the
Chairperson;
(b) seven representatives of Government
departments in the district;
(c) Members of Parliament in the district;
(d) a representative of the Zambia Red Cross
Society;
(e) a representative each of the non-
governmental organisations and
community-based organisations involved
in disaster management in the district;
46 No. 2 of 2026] Disaster Management (Amendment)
(f)a representative of a religious organisation
in the district; and
(g) one person from the private sector with
relevant knowledge and experience in
matters relating to this Act; and
(c) subsection (3) and the substitution therefor of the following:
(3) The District Disaster Risk
Management Co-ordinator shall be the
secretary of the District Committee.
Amendment 18. Section 24 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion
of section 24 of subsection (3) and the substitution therefor of the following:
(3) The District Disaster Risk Management
Co-ordinator shall exercise the powers under this Act
in consultation with the Division, Zambia Police
Service, national security services and any other
institution involved in disaster risk management in the
district.
Amendment 19. Section 37(1) of the principal Act is amended by the deletion
of section 37 of paragraph (l) and the substitution therefor of the following:
(l) emergency procurement procedures in
Act No. 8 of accordance with the Public Procurement Act,
2020 2020;.
Repeal and 20. The principal Act is amended in Part VII by the deletion of

sections.title: Section 42(2) of the principal Act is amended by the— Amendment
sections.body:
of section 42
(a) insertion of the following new paragraph immediately after
paragraph (d):
(e) the minimum relief requirements in humanitarian
response to a disaster;; and
(b) renumbering of paragraphs (e) and (f) as paragraphs (f)
and (g), respectively.

sections.title: The principal Act is amended by the deletion of the— General
sections.body:
(a) words “Disaster Management Co-ordinator” wherever amendment
the words appear and the substitution therefor of the
words “Disaster Risk Management Co-ordinator”;
(b) words “disaster management plan” wherever the words
appear and the substitution therefor of the words
“disaster risk management plan”;
(c) words “national disaster management plan” wherever the
words appear and the substitution therefor of the words
“national disaster risk management plan”;
(d) words “sector disaster management plan” wherever the
words appear and the substitution therefor of the words
“sector disaster risk management plan”; and
(e) word “Unit” wherever the word appears and the
substitution therefor of the word “Division”.
